name: The Ultimate Cheesecake- Mccalls Cooking Sch
recipeIngredient: 5 packages (8 oz size) cream cheese at room temperature
1 package (10 oz) frozen raspberries, in light syrup, thawed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Make crust: In a medium bowl, using a fork, toss crumbs with butter and sugar until moistened. Pour into a 10-inch springform pan. With the back of a fork, press mixture evenly over bottom and up sides of the pan. Bake for 5 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on a wire rack.
3. Increase oven temperature to 450°F.
4. Make filling: In a large bowl of electric mixer, beat cream cheese, sugar, flour, lemon zest, orange zest, and vanilla at high speed until blended. Beat in eggs and egg yolks one at a time; beat until smooth, scraping the bowl with a spatula occasionally. Beat in heavy cream. Pour filling into prepared pan. Bake for 10 minutes.
5. Lower oven temperature to 250°F. Bake for 1 hour longer or until center is set but not firm. Remove from oven and let cool on a rack for 2 hours.
6. With the back of a spoon, spread sour cream evenly over the top of the cake to within 1/2 inch of the edge. Refrigerate for 3 hours or overnight.
7. In a food processor, puree raspberries with their syrup; pour through a sieve placed over a medium-sized saucepan. Discard seeds. Mix cornstarch with the puree until blended. Bring to boiling, stirring constantly; simmer for 1 minute or until the sauce is thickened and clear. Pour raspberry sauce into a small bowl. Cover and refrigerate until cold.
8. In a clean food processor, puree peach slices with 1/4 cup sugar until blended. Pour into a small bowl; set aside.
9. Remove cake from the pan and place on a serving dish. Spoon raspberry sauce over sour cream, covering half of the surface. Spread peach puree on the other half.
10. Arrange kiwifruit and peach slices along the line where the raspberry sauce meets the peach puree. Add whole raspberries as desired. Cut the cake into wedges to serve. Pass remaining raspberry sauce and peach puree.
Variation: Add variety to recipes that call for graham cracker crusts by mixing in the flavor of your favorite cookie. In any graham cracker crust recipe, use half graham cracker crumbs and half chocolate, vanilla, or shortbread cookie crumbs for an interesting version of an old favorite.
